hero.description: Software Engineer with hands-on experience building resilient backend architectures, AI-driven SaaS platforms, distributed systems, and cloud infrastructure at scale.

exp1.title: Cloud Developer
exp1.description: At Oryza Labs (Tecnopuc), architected 15+ distributed systems on AWS for enterprise clients including Sem Parar and Shopee. End-to-end responsibility from system design to production deployment, bridging software engineering and operational cloud infrastructure.

exp2.title: DevOps Developer
exp2.description: At Beasybox, promoted after 4 months to lead infrastructure, CI/CD, and system performance. Implemented CI/CD pipelines with Docker, structured cloud infrastructure from scratch, and optimized AI response latency by ~40%. Scaled the platform to 100+ companies.

exp3.title: Backend Developer
exp3.description: At Beasybox, started the SaaS from scratch, built the engineering team, and structured processes for 8 people. Led the architectural shift from single-model AI to Multi-Agent Orchestration and engineered a RAG architecture with PGVector and PostgreSQL.

exp4.title: Vice President
exp4.description: At PUCRS Liga de Algoritmos, built the league's website and platform from scratch, coordinated weekly competitive programming challenges, and grew the league from 10 to 80 active members (700% growth).

exp5.title: Math Tutor
exp5.description: Internship at Colégio Marista Champagnat, assisting students with calculus and academic project management.

sections.projects_desc: Here are some of the works I developed. Focused on performance, user experience, and clean code.

beasybot.description_short: An external AI agent acting as a senior consultant, centralizing and processing all company knowledge.
beasybot.description_long_p1: An external AI agent acting as a senior consultant, centralizing and processing all company knowledge — including meeting minutes, documents, and strategic reports.
beasybot.description_long_p2: My responsibility in the project was comprehensive, covering much of the development pipeline. I acted from scope definition to deployment on AWS.

donation.title: Donation Platform
donation.description_short: Application facilitating donations to the Pão dos Pobres institution, allowing simple and secure contributions.
donation.description_long_p1: Application that facilitates donations to the Pão dos Pobres institution, allowing users to contribute simply and securely.
donation.description_long_p2: Although my initial focus was Frontend with React and ShadCn, I assumed additional responsibility for integration with AWS services (S3, SES).

meeting.description_short: AI meeting recorder that transcribes, summarizes, and highlights key points in real-time.
meeting.description_long_p1: My own AI-powered meeting recorder that transcribes, summarizes, and highlights key points from meetings in real-time.
meeting.description_long_p2: I simulated a graphical environment inside an EC2 using Xvfb, allowing Selenium to operate Google Meet without a real graphical interface.

lobo.description_long_p1: Lobo Guará was an initiative aimed at helping migrants in Brazil. The idea emerged during the 5th PUCRS Innovation Marathon.
lobo.description_long_p2: I mainly worked on building the application interface to strengthen my front-end knowledge.

beecrm.description_short: Full-featured CRM with Kanban pipelines, integrated email inbox, contact management, and meeting scheduling.
beecrm.description_long_p1: BeeCRM is a complete customer relationship management system built with NestJS on the backend and React on the frontend. It features drag-and-drop Kanban pipelines for sales, an integrated email inbox, contact management, and calendar scheduling.
beecrm.description_long_p2: I worked on both backend and frontend, handling JWT authentication, AWS service integrations (S3, SES, Bedrock), WebSockets for real-time features, and Prisma with PostgreSQL for the database layer.

numerama.description_short: Native Android POS system for payment terminals, built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose.
numerama.description_long_p1: Numerama is a native Android Point-of-Sale (POS) system designed for payment terminals. Built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose for a declarative and modern UI.
numerama.description_long_p2: The project follows Clean Architecture with Dagger Hilt for dependency injection, Retrofit for API integration, and reactive state management using ViewModel and StateFlow.

liga.description_short: PUCRS competitive programming platform to solve algorithm challenges and track rankings.
liga.description_long_p1: The Liga de Algoritmos Platform is the frontend for PUCRS's competitive programming system, where members solve algorithm challenges and track rankings in real-time.
liga.description_long_p2: Built with React, TypeScript, and Vite, the platform includes a problem-solving interface, leaderboards, user profiles, and an admin panel for problem management.

projects.all_projects_desc: A detailed collection of the works I developed, focused on solving real problems with clean and efficient code.

stack.description: Tools and technologies I use daily.

bento.description: Find me on social media or send a message.

who_am_i.title: Who Am I?
who_am_i.p1: Hi! I'm Bernardo Kirsch, a Software Engineer with hands-on experience building resilient backend architectures, AI-driven SaaS platforms, distributed systems, and cloud infrastructure at scale. My focus is on taking extreme ownership of products, designing scalable architectures, and turning complex bottlenecks into performant, reliable solutions.
who_am_i.p2: Throughout my career, I've operated across the entire lifecycle of software development, with a heavy emphasis on cloud architecture (AWS), DevOps automation, AI orchestration, and building production-critical systems for high-scale applications.

education.title: College and Education
education.p1: Currently, I am studying Software Engineering at PUC-RS and Applied Mathematics at UFRGS, combining logic and technology to solve complex problems.
education.p2: Before that, I completed high school at Pastor Dohms College, where I developed a solid foundation in exact sciences and the German language.

hobbies.title: Beyond Code
hobbies.p1: When I'm not coding, I love going out with my friends and discovering new places. I believe exploring the world around me inspires me.
hobbies.p2: I also love music, which helps me stay focused. There's nothing better than coffee, code, and Bad Bunny on headphones.

certs.cert3_desc: Complete fullstack web development course, covering from frontend with React to backend with Node.js.
